title: Recognize response (design 0004 v1 — DRAFT, non-normative)
description: Draft wire contract for the recognize capability's serve provider (design 0004 §5): the JSON a served recognizer returns from POST /recognize. Non-normative while the design is Proposed; the normative copy lands under spec/ with the v1 spec deltas.

recognizer.description: Recognizer identity, declared by the backend (design 0004 §4): feeds the derivation identity of the persisted annotation representation.

entities.description: Entity dictionary for ids referenced by annotations[].entities. Ids are free-form but should be stable across the corpus (open question, design 0004 §6).

items.description: One recognized statement with a time span. The span maps directly to a persisted `time` citation, so it MUST satisfy 0 <= start_s <= end_s. A zero-length span (end_s == start_s) is permitted and denotes an instantaneous event (e.g. a single pitch-release frame). JSON Schema (2020-12) cannot express the cross-field `end_s >= start_s` bound portably, so the ingestion side rejects a reversed span as a malformed record (design 0004 §5); the per-field `minimum: 0` below is the portion the schema can enforce.

end_s.description: Span end in seconds (float). MUST be >= start_s; a reversed span is rejected at ingestion (see the item description).

event.description: Producer-defined event vocabulary (e.g. "pitch", "at_bat", "appearance"). Not enumerated by the contract.

entities.description: Entity ids involved; each should resolve in the top-level entities dictionary.

text.description: The human-readable statement. This is what gets chunked and indexed, so retrieval quality is bounded by its quality. MUST be non-empty and not whitespace-only (minLength + the `\S` pattern), so a blank statement can never enter the index as a non-searchable chunk.

sources.description: Recognizer tags that contributed (e.g. "scorebug", "face"). Provenance metadata, not a relevance signal (design 0004 §4).
